properties.remark: LAND SPOUT TORNADO REPORTED WITH MULTIPLE PHOTOS. WAS ON GROUND FOR AROUND 5 MINUTES.
POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE REPORTED AT FARM WITH BARN DOOR BLOWN OUT AND MULTIPLE WINDOWS BLOWN OUT. PHOTO ALSO SHOWS POSSIBLE TORNADO. DAMAGE SURVEY PLANNED ON MONDAY MARCH 19TH.

THIS TORNADO BEGAN AT 914 PM CDT ABOUT 2 MILES NORTHWEST OF GLAZIER, TEXAS IN HEMPHILL COUNTY AND ENDED AT 922 PM CDT APPROXIMATELY 2.9 MILES NORTH OF GLAZIER, TEXAS IN HEMPHILL COUNTY. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 2.8 MILES AND A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 70 YARDS. IT CAUSED DAMAGE TO TREES, POWER LINES, AND AN OUT BUILDING. THIS TORNADO WAS RATED EF-1 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 80 TO 90 MPH.

THIS BRIEF TORNADO BEGAN AT 1021 PM CDT 2 MILES NORTHWEST OF WHEELER, TEXAS AND ENDED AT 1022 PM CDT APPROXIMATELY 1 MILE NORTHWEST OF WHEELER, TEXAS. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 0.6 MILE AND A PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. THIS TORNADO WAS RATED EF-0 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 80 TO 85 MPH. EF-0 DAMAGE OCCURRED TO TREES, BARNS, AND 5 HOMES. ONE MANUFACTURED HOME WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED. THE OTHER HOMES HAD ROOF DAMAGE.
*** 1 INJ *** 5 PEOPLE TRAPPED IN A HOME, ROOF COLLAPSED. ONE HOUSE DESTOYED IN NORTHWEST WHEELER. TREE FELL ON VEHICLE TRAPPING TWO PEOPLE. ONE PERSON TRANSPORTED TO HOSPITAL WITH INJURIES.
